subject: feat(layout): wire BurgerMenu into header, retire inline sign-out buttons
body:
- AppLayout: BurgerMenu mounts trailing-right in the header after
  NotificationBell. Single nav entry point across all viewports —
  replaces both the desktop "Sign out" text button and the mobile sign-out
  icon button. APP_NAME pulled from constants/app.ts so the header label
  is owned by one source (was hardcoded "Sancio").
- Menu items (deviates from spec's "placeholders in Chunk 8" — wiring
  real functionality since the dependencies exist and "do the proper
  solution" is the session rule):
    * Dark mode toggle — label flips between "Dark mode" / "Light mode"
      with sun/moon icon. aria-label reads "Switch to X mode". Uses
      keepOpen so users can toggle back and forth without re-opening.
      Fires applyTheme() from the theme module.
    * Install app — highlighted item, visible only when the app can be
      installed (canInstallNative || needsManualInstructions) and not
      already installed. Chromium → installNative() from usePWAInstall.
      Safari / Firefox → opens the existing InstallInstructionsModal.
    * Sign out — destructive (red), visible only when AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED
      is off. Fires logout().
- InstallInstructionsModal now mounts at the layout level so the
  BurgerMenu action and the PwaInstallPrompt banner share the same modal
  component.
- Items array is useMemo'd on its inputs (dark, install state, logout)
  so BurgerMenu gets a stable reference across unrelated parent re-renders
  (notifications, route changes).
- CLAUDE.md → Adopted Patterns gains a BURGER_MENU — implemented entry
  with rationale, file map, deviations from the generic pattern, and the
  keepOpen design note.

Tags: feature, layout, burger-menu, theme, pwa
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: burger-menu
Semver: minor
title: wire BurgerMenu into header, retire inline sign-out buttons
